title: Spree River Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **The Spree River helped create Berlin:** the old twin towns of Berlin and Coelln grew around the river, its island and its bridge crossings.
**Museum Island exists because of the Spree:** the river and its channels created the island setting for Prussia’s museum complex.
**The best central Spree views are on foot:** Liebknecht Bridge, Friedrichsbruecke, Museum Island and Nikolaiviertel explain the historic centre clearly.
**During the Cold War, parts of the Spree became part of the border landscape,** turning water that once connected the city into a line of division.
**A Spree boat tour is useful as a second perspective,** especially after walking the historic centre first.
